metadata.courseDescription: Replace this with your course description. This template generates an interactive course microsite from structured JSON data.

site.positioningCallout: Replace this with a short statement about what makes this course distinctive and how it fits in your program.
site.nonTechnicalNote: Replace this with any prerequisites or background information students should know before starting.
site.unitRationale: Replace this with a brief explanation of the pedagogical reasoning behind your unit structure.

learningOutcomes.outcome: Demonstrate understanding of core course concepts through applied projects.
Analyze and evaluate creative work using discipline-specific criteria.
Produce portfolio-ready artifacts that integrate course themes.

units.description: Apply concepts to new contexts and challenge assumptions.
Synthesize learning into more complex outputs.
Produce polished work and articulate your process.

courseStructure.cognitiveProgression: The course moves from foundational skills through exploration and integration to final synthesis.

weeks.overview: Welcome to the course. This week establishes the core framework and vocabulary you will use throughout the quarter.
learningObjectives: Understand the course structure and expectations

activities.description: Course structure, expectations, and the quarter roadmap.
Hands-on exercise introducing the course theme.
Configure your workspace and tools for the quarter.

weeks.lookingAhead: Next week we begin working with core methods.

projects.overview: Apply foundational concepts to produce your first course artifact.

aiUse.restrictions: No AI tools for this project. Establish your own baseline first.

projects.overview: Expand your methods by exploring new perspectives and tools.

aiUse.restrictions: AI tools permitted for research and ideation. Document all AI use.

projects.overview: Synthesize learning from the first two units into a more complex output.

aiUse.restrictions: Full AI collaboration permitted. Maintain clear documentation of human vs. AI contributions.

projects.overview: Present your best work and reflect on your growth across the quarter.

aiUse.restrictions: All tools available. Final presentation must demonstrate your own voice and judgment.

elements: My stance on AI in my creative practice
positionStatement.description: A living document that records your evolving position on AI use in your work.

aiUseLog.description: Track every AI interaction, what you accepted, what you rejected, and why.

fields: Why I rejected or revised it
recordOfResistance.description: Document moments where you deliberately chose a different path than AI recommended.

unitProgression.guidance: Establish your own baseline without AI augmentation.
AI tools allowed for research and ideation. Document all use.
Use AI as a creative collaborator. Maintain clear authorship.
All tools available. Your judgment determines what you use and how.

principles: AI is a tool, not a collaborator with agency. You remain the author.
Document every AI interaction so your process is transparent and defensible.
The goal is not to avoid AI but to use it with intention and awareness.

policies.lateWork: Late submissions receive a 10% penalty per day. Extensions require advance communication with the instructor.
policies.attendance: Attendance is expected for all sessions. More than two unexcused absences may affect your grade.
